EROL KELEŞ (Elâzığ) - Siz konuşurken biz sesimizi çıkarmadık ya! Ya, lütfen ama! Biz mi terk edelim burayı? Saygılı olun lütfen ya!
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Ne dediysem "Bu böyle değil, bu böyle değil."
BAŞKAN MÜŞERREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Size saygım ve sevgim sonsuz ama tutup da siyasi bir meseleyi burada tartışarak çocuklarımıza haksızlık ederiz. Biz başka bir meseleyle toplandık.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisi siyasi bir yerdir.
ALİ İNCİ (Sakarya) - Ekranı açıyorsun, ekrana konuşuyorsun sanki biz burada yokmuş gibi, bu da hoş bir şey değil.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Niye, size de baktım.
records.englishTranslation:
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Welcome, thank you for your presentations.
I will start with TURKSTAT, I have a few comments and questions, then I will move on to the Ministry of Justice.
First of all, as a member of the main opposition party, I would like to emphasize once again that since the transition to a one-man regime regarding TUIK, all possible criticisms of your institution have been made, every word that could be said has been said, that is, this institution is not an autonomous institution. There is actually a data kitchen here, the data kitchen of the palace; I'll start by noting this. From inflation to unemployment, poverty, education data, we, our citizens and all of us, see how these data are.
Now, if we turn to children, according to your data, the number of children dragged into crime will be 179 thousand in 2023, rising to 202 thousand in 2024.
There is a 13 percent increase, but we cannot see that you are actually transparently publishing the vital correlations such as family income levels and parental unemployment of these children who are dragged into crime. If you publish these, it would actually be possible to get to the root of the social problem, but by not publishing them, getting to the root of this is prevented; I have to say this.
According to your data for 2023, the number of children coming or brought to security units increases by 10 percent compared to the previous year, reaching 606 thousand, and around 242 thousand of these children come because of being dragged into crime. Your data. This increase is actually an undisguised consequence of a direct correlation between poverty and crime. Dear MPs, Türkiye ranks first in Europe in terms of child poverty. According to EUROSTAT data, Türkiye is the country with the highest rate of children at risk of poverty and social exclusion among European countries, with 41.3 percent, and this rate is 2 times the European Union average; The European Union average is around 24 percent, and interestingly, even in TÜİK's own data, we see that -2023- 31.3 percent of children live in households suffering from severe financial deprivation.
Now why did I tell you this? Because we say that there is a link between crime and poverty, the equation is very simple; If you condemn the family to hunger and the mercy of social aid, it is difficult to prevent that child from being dragged into crime; These children, whose hope, future, or dreams have been stolen, can and do seek their survival reflex in mafia relationships, drug gangs, and illegal relationships. Unfortunately, as TÜİK distorts the data, we cannot get to the root of this huge problem; from inflation and unemployment. There is no need to re-enter these data, the data is hidden, the data is obscured, but as long as it continues to do this, TÜİK continues to be an accomplice to this crime.
Now I know the answers to some of these questions, but I still want to ask: Why don't you transparently publish the correlation between the reasons why children are dragged into crime and the income level of families, housing status and parental unemployment, or why don't you conduct a study on this? If you did, please explain. Again, according to the data of the Ministry of National Education, the number of students working within the scope of MESEM has exceeded 1.5 million.

Unfortunately, child labor has been legalized under the name of "MESEM" and how many children have fallen into the streets and into crime due to this system; Do you have any work on this? Every child who drops out of school becomes a part of your statistics, and why doesn't TÜİK publish the educational status of these children in detail? Where is your deep poverty data? TurkStat's hiding of the poverty line and hunger line under the name of "change in calculation method" actually covers up the bread stolen from children's plates. Look, according to PISA data, 19.3 percent of students in Türkiye declare that they skip meals at least once a week due to lack of money. This data is proof that the economic crisis directly affects children's right to development and, indeed, equality of opportunity in education. If 1 in 5 children in Türkiye goes to school hungry today, to what extent is the risk of these children being dragged into crime really affected? We need to ask this together.
Therefore, why are you disclosing the statistics regarding the security units, the children coming to the security units - you gave the figures here, they are also on your website - but with a delay, why don't you disclose them on time, at the beginning of each year, at the end of the year, so that we, as politicians, can develop policy suggestions or the Government can do its duty and develop policies?
Why is the increase in the types of crimes involving children, especially substance use and gang-related crimes, diluted in the 2024-25 data? After what filters are these figures passed through, for example, do they come before this Commission?
Therefore, TÜİK said, "Poverty has fallen." “Poverty has fallen,” he says. As I say, children's wards in prisons are overflowing. We need to stop TUIK from being a statistical institution and turning it into the data office of the government, because otherwise this institution will be remembered as the "thief of the future" in the public's conscience.
will continue.
Now, let's start from the 40th page of your presentation: You say that the number of children taken into custody due to drug use and stimulant use has doubled compared to the previous year. Now, is this increase concentrated in a certain socioeconomic group, or is there a spread throughout society? This is the first one. How do you interpret this?
Secondly, we see that children are mostly brought to crime units for injury and theft. What does this say about the link between youth violence and poverty?
Again, you mentioned immigrants, but in your data, how many of the children who are dragged into crime or mentioned as victims are immigrants or children under temporary protection? We would like to ask you to explain this as well.
Again, what is the reflection of the 2-fold increase in drug-related crimes among immigrant children? Let's ask for this too.
Finally, "In 2024 - page 41 of your presentation - 279,620 children were brought to security units as victims." It is said. I wonder how much of this victimization is due to domestic violence, how much of it is due to cyber bullying or abuse; Do you have any data regarding this? If there is, please explain, but I don't think there is much. Otherwise, please work on this.

These are my comments about TURKSTAT, should I continue with the Ministry of Justice? Let me continue, okay.
As for the Ministry of Justice, one of the reasons why our Commission was established was that the public debate about children dragged into crime came to the fore as a result of the reactions on social media. As we all know, it started to be discussed intensively with the Minguzzi murder. As our Ministry of Justice always does - justice in Turkey - first an incident occurs, reactions grow, when there are public reactions, the government says that a regulation will be made and the Ministry of Justice brings judicial packages upon these reactions. Again, we all knew that there was a regulation in the draft of the eleventh judicial package regarding children dragged into crime, in fact, this debate started intensely with the Minguzzi murder. What was this regulation included in the draft? Let's remember: It was about increasing prison sentences for children between the ages of 15-18. There were reactions from the public, and as an avalanche of reactions grew on social media, this regulation was removed from the package for now. Therefore, we say that there is a clear approach problem here; On the one hand, experts have been saying "restorative justice" for decades and years, and on the other hand, the Ministry says "More punishment, more imprisonment." he says. Therefore, I ask, who judges children with the same logic as adults, puts them in prison, that is, actually makes them more accustomed to crime in bad conditions? I want to ask.
I continue.
We all know that organized crime organizations are expanding day by day, both numerically and geographically, in the areas of serious crime such as drug trafficking, looting, extortion, smuggling, laundering of proceeds of crime, murder, and wounding, and they continue to expand. For example, England defines children used by gangs for drugs and crime as victims, not perpetrators; When a child is caught, for example, instead of handcuffing him, the police take him under the protection umbrella of social services. In our country, while some of the leaders of criminal organizations are free, our children face heavy penalties in prisons.
CHAIRMAN MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - For information.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - No, no, I said it as an expression, in general. I understand
This year in our country.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Let's give correct information. Let's have an informed discussion.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Let me continue, you will answer, Mr. President.
These structures, these organized crime gangs, through social media - I think someone just mentioned it in their presentation - seriously
They continue to make propaganda and easily reach our children in neighborhoods living in poverty and exclusion and direct them to crime. Therefore, is it really a deterrent or compatible with the sense of justice if our children who are drawn into these organizations are sentenced to heavy penalties as you think in your draft? We need to question this together within the Commission's work.
Another issue is prisons. You know, we say that every decision made about a child determines his or her entire life.

When we look at the statistics about children, we see that, unfortunately, children are literally growing up in the corridors of the courthouse, that is, in prison. Now, according to your November 2025 data, more than 4,600 children are imprisoned in Türkiye. Some children are also held in adult prisons, right? Did I just hear that correctly? You also had such data. Or is it kept?
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Age 18.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - I know, but because there is no space. Is it true?
MINISTRY OF JUSTICE GENERAL DIRECTOR OF PRISON AND DETENTION HOUSES ENIS YAVUZ YILDIRIM - In individual departments.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - OK, but there is. I know it's separate, but I'm asking if it exists.
Now, from the New Zealand model. Maybe these models can be worked on. In the New Zealand model, the child meets with the victim, family and social workers before appearing before the judge, a plan is made on how to compensate for the damage caused by the child instead of punishment, and when you look at it, New Zealand is at the top of the countries that reduce the rate of children going to prison and the rate of repeat crime to the lowest levels in the world.
We're looking at the UK and Ireland. So why am I telling these? I am explaining this so that we can discuss how we can take our children out of prisons with the restorative justice system around the world, instead of increasing the penalties for those aged 15-18. When we look at the UK and Ireland, again, the child is identified in the risk group between the ages of 8-11, before he even encounters crime, and is supported by a multi-stakeholder network, especially by units such as the municipality, school and security. In this country, we say, "Let's increase the punishment." Then the world - as I said - asks "How can we keep the child away from the system?" he tries.
The last example is Norway and Sweden. In these countries, juvenile prisons and care homes are not only inspected by ministries, but also by independent and broadly authorized juvenile ombudsmen.
Therefore, as a result of a defendant-focused, punitive approach, we know that children are all in prisons; There are children who are abused, exposed to physical and psychological violence, and while they are victims of these crimes, unfortunately, they can also become perpetrators because the ground is prepared for their association with criminal organizations. I say this to emphasize the importance of the victim and perpetrator position.
What I just mentioned, for example, these child ombudsman monitoring mechanisms in Norway and Sweden. Why, for example, are not human rights violations in our juvenile prisons inspected by independent committees? Is there such a study? It could be.
Most of the children who are dragged into crime come from the crime of wounding. How many of these children have been rehabilitated, how many of them have picked up a knife again when they went out on the streets, and how many have committed crimes again? Do you have any data on this? Why can't the integration of Forensic Medicine and social services be established?

Finally, the last two questions: The data presented by your Ministry - I couldn't find the page now, but - Mr. President also said - you have inter-institutional protocols, that is, you say "inter-institutional coordination to keep children away from crime" between the Ministry of Family and the Ministry of National Education, but when we look at the figures, there are also serious increases, you talk about decreases between some periods, but do you put the responsibility for this increase only on the judicial system, or do you see a deficiency in the social state policy as well?
Again, within the scope of this MESEM, within the framework of your Ministry's protocols with the Ministry of National Education, what independent control mechanisms do you have or do you have to prevent children from being used as cheap labor in the industry?
Do you have such a mechanism? If you say that too.
My last question is a bit political, but with Law No. 5395, which is the definition of child, we define a person who has not reached the age of 18 as a child, but with the MESEM application, we impose worker responsibilities on these children at an early age. Don't you think this contradicts the "best interest of the child" principle in the spirit of the law?
Thanks.
PRESIDENT MUŞARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Now there is a request for a speech, I would like to make a small reminder to our esteemed deputy before giving the floor. Our esteemed representative has of course touched on very important questions and important issues, and he would like to make important contributions. Of course, everyone is excited and all their questions are sometimes related to political decisions or issues. We will base the policy recommendations on the data that will emerge as a result of the work of this Commission by asking "Do you think about this, do you think about that?" More interlocutors will come to ask.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - We decide how we want to talk, that is, we talk what we want.
PRESIDENT MUŞARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Of course, you can talk as you want, but there is also a situation like this.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - So this is not a university. This is a political place.
CHAIRMAN MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - The purpose of this meeting.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - I know the purpose of the meeting, and I decide how I want to talk.
EROL KELEŞ (Elâzığ) - If you allow him, let him talk. Look, nobody said anything while you were talking.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - I can talk as I want.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - This is not a debate club.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - I can talk as I want, I cannot ask you how to talk.
EROL KELEŞ (Elâzığ) - But please do not intervene.
PRESIDENT MUSARRAF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - But I will say something.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - I really don't want to make a polemic.
EROL KELEŞ (Elâzığ) - Should we have answered too!
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - But let us decide how we will talk.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - We cannot get results without methodology, so this is not teaching, it is scientific.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - We decide how we want to talk, otherwise we will leave.
PRESIDENT MUŞERREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - You know, the scientific thought that Atatürk suggested to us, that is exactly the scientific thought that will raise it above the level of contemporary civilization, we can produce benefits for children by working with a certain methodology.

I find the questions you ask very valuable, but the addressee.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - That's why I said, we would be more comfortable if he was the Minister of Justice.
PRESIDENT MUSHARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - I have already told you that the Minister of Justice will come, I have already told you that the Minister of Justice will come. But in the methodology, we said that the first two weeks will analyze the risk factors and the situation.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Shouldn't we talk? Then, let's go and let them report to us.
ALİ İNCİ (Sakarya) - For God's sake, do not use this word!
CHAIRMAN MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Oh, speak, but Mr. Deputy, speak. Dear Deputy, I can listen to you for hours, what do you mean?
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Okay, don't tell us how to talk.
ALİ İNCİ (Sakarya) - Well, we are here too.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - I'm asking you.
ALİ İNCİ (Sakarya) - But there is no need to listen there.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - How we talk and how we ask questions.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - I would like to request you to use your time economically and try to comply with the methodology as much as possible.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Look, let's do it like this: Let them send us the information note, we will ask them questions. I am already asking questions through a motion, but I cannot get an answer to any of them, and we are in this situation.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - You are not asking your interlocutor right now.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Well, okay, then they shouldn't come, why are we here if there is no addressee? Don't do it, I beg you!
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - All your interlocutors will come within the framework of a methodology, we told you.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Well, let's do it this way: Give us which questions we should ask, in which methodology.
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Give the questions too.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Now, okay, just a little reminder.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Let them prepare the answers, so let's do it that way.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Dear Deputy, please.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - So you are making me do something. I'm asking you!
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - But you are also interfering with our rights. I made a request to move forward in a method circle, there is nothing personally about you here.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Please, do not interfere with anyone, I beg you, act impartially.
PRESIDENT MUŞARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Impartiality actually requires sticking to the framework.
GİZEM ÖZCAN (Muğla) - In order to close this issue and contribute, I will say this.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - I will say one more sentence, then I will speak in order.
GİZEM ÖZCAN (Muğla) - OK.
PRESIDENT MUŞARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - This is not a one-man system, it is a Presidential government system.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - That's what we say, you can fix it.
PRESIDENT MUŞARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - And our President was elected with over 50 percent of the votes. So, this is not a place for political polemics, nor is it a debate club.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Now, me.
EROL KELEŞ (Elâzığ) - Dear Deputy, please but, please but. Let him finish his sentence, please.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - He corrects whatever I said.

EROL KELEŞ (Elâzığ) - We didn't raise our voices while you were talking! Oh, please! Should we leave this place? Be respectful please!
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Whatever I said, "This is not like this, this is not like that."
MAYOR MUŞARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - I have endless respect and love for you, but we would be doing an injustice to our children by discussing a political issue here. We gathered with another issue.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - The Grand National Assembly of Türkiye is a political place.
ALİ İNCİ (Sakarya) - You turn on the screen, you talk to the screen as if we are not here, and this is not a nice thing.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Why, I looked at you too.

records.title: Emniyet Genel Müdürlüğü ve Jandarma Genel Komutanlığı temsilcilerinin yaptıkları sunumlara ilişkin görüşme münasebetiyle

fields.Subject: Emniyet Genel Müdürlüğü ve Jandarma Genel Komutanlığı temsilcilerinin yaptıkları sunumlara ilişkin görüşme münasebetiyle

records.originalTurkish:
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Teşekkürler sunumlarınız için.
Şimdi, yani gerçekten hepimiz sokakta büyüyen, yoksulluk içinde yaşayan, ailesi dağılmış bu çocuklarımızı ilk kez ne zaman görüyoruz? Onları bir parkta, bir okulda ya da bir sosyal hizmet merkezinde mi görüyoruz yoksa ilk temas noktası karakol mu oluyor? Ne yazık ki yanıtımız karakol. Yani bugün Emniyet kayıtlarında, sizin de verilerinizde on binlerce çocuk var. Verileri tekrar tek tek anlatmayacağım ama 2024 verilerinize göre güvenlik birimlerine gelen çocuk sayısı bir yılda yüzde 10 artmış ve 600 bini geçmiş, çocuk sayısından bahsediyorum. Yani "Çocukların okullarının önünde -burada var- uyuşturucu operasyonları yaptık." diye anlatıyorsunuz ama uyuşturucu satıcılarının kucağına düşerken bu çocuklar -bir yandan da açıp bakın telefonlarınızda- yok Daltonlar, yok Barış Boyun Çetesi, yok Redkit -reklamlarını da yapmak istemiyorum ama- gibi çeteler 15 yaşındaki çocukları sosyal medyadan âdeta tetikçi olarak devşiriyor. Sorum şu: Önleyici polislik nerede? Yani burada önerilerinizin ilki koruyucu ve önleyici mekanizmaların güçlendirilmesinin zorunlu olduğu. Peki, sizin önleyici politika öneriniz ne Emniyet olarak? Çünkü Emniyetin görev tanımı sadece "yakala, getir, teslim et" bunlar olamaz. Bugün dünyada -az önce TÜİK sunumunda da bahsettim, farklı ülkelerden örnekler verdim- çocukları suçtan uzak tutacak önleyici sosyal projeler, politikalar konuşuluyor. Tabii, bu sadece Emniyetimizi tek başına ilgilendiren bir mesele değil, aslında Emniyetimizi aşan, aslında siyasi irade isteyen şeyler. O yüzden burada kimseyi de zan altında bırakmak, Emniyet teşkilatımızı, Jandarma teşkilatımızı zan altında bırakmak istemem. Burada siyasi iradeye bağlı bir eksiklik olduğunu vurgulamam gerekiyor.
Birkaç soru şöyle: Emniyet sunumunuzda 49'uncu sayfanızdaki tablonuzda topluma karşı işlenen suçların 2021'de 10.307 iken 2025'te 17.964'e çıktığını görüyoruz. Beş yılda yüzde 75'e yakın bu korkunç artışın ana sebebi
sizce ne? Yani "Şunları yaptık." diye istatistikleri veriyorsunuz ama ben sonuçta Emniyet teşkilatımızdan kendi fikirlerini de öğrenmek istiyorum. Yine, onun üstündeki tabloda -"Kişilere Karşı İşlenen Suçlar" tablonuz- şimdi siz "2024-2025 artışı yok, hatta yüzde 2 de azalma var." yazmışsınız ama 2021'de 64 bin küsurmuş bu suçların sayısı, 2025'te 90 binlere çıkmış; siz burada sadece son bir yıldaki düşüşü yazmışsınız. O yüzden buradaki gerçeği hep beraber farkına varalım diye, tutanaklara da geçmesi açısından söylemek istiyorum.
Şimdi "Sokaklarda rekor uyuşturucu operasyonları yapıyoruz." diyorsunuz. Doğrudur, yapıyorsunuzdur ama çocukların bu bataklığa düşme oranının her yıl katlanarak artmasını nasıl açıklıyorsunuz? Okul çevrelerindeki bu uyuşturucu satışları neden bitirilemiyor? Yani buna ilişkin sizin fikirleriniz, sizin deneyimleriniz nedir?
Yine, raporunuzda 4,4 milyondan fazla kişiye güvenli internet eğitimi verdiğinizi söylüyorsunuz. Peki, bu eğitimlere rağmen yasa dışı bahis, siber riskler kapsamındaki topluma karşı işlenmiş suçlar giderek ve hızla artıyor. Bunun sebebi ne? Yani biz, ne güzel, bu kadar, 4,4 milyon kişiye güvenli internet eğitimi veriyoruz, neden artıyor o zaman? Yani siz burada birkaç yorumda bulundunuz internet kullanımındaki sıkıntılar vesaire ama -tekrar söyleyeyim reklamlarını yapmadan- ben az önce çıkıp baktım, yani o çetelerin isimlerini buraya girdiğinizde, YouTube'a, internette herhangi bir siteye girdiğinizde hepsini görebiliyorsunuz. Yani çok özür diliyorum, çocuklar tetikçi olarak devşiriliyor, özendiriliyor. Neden bu siteler kapatılmıyor? Mesela bizim Cumhurbaşkanı adayımızın sitesi kapatılabiliyor, sosyal medya hesabı kapatılabiliyor kolaylıkla ya da bir genç "tweet" attığı için özgürlüğünden mahrum edilebiliyor, bir gece yarısı ters kelepçeyle alınabiliyor ama bu çeteler, bu kötü reklamlar yani bu kötü suça teşvik eden konular YouTube'da, internette durmaya devam ediyor. Emniyet Teşkilatımız bununla ilgili ne yapıyor, bunu merak ediyorum ya da ne yapabilir, neyi eksik buluyor ya da yapamıyor? Sosyal medyada çocukları suça özendiren bu suç makinaları neden engellenemiyor, neden engellenemiyor?
Son olarak, tablonuzda yine 2021'den bu yana ateşli silahlarda yüzde 100'e yakın bir artış söz konusu. Burada acaba çocukların eline bu silahlar nasıl geçiyor? Bununla ilgili bir çalışmanız, veriniz var mı?
Son sorum: Kayıp çocuk vakalarındaki artış trendleri. Bu, çocukların suç örgütlerinin eline düşme riskini ne ölçüde tetikliyor? Bununla ilgili bir çalışmanız var mı?
Son olarak da, Jandarma kırsal bölgelerimize bakıyor, kırsalda da suça sürüklenen çocuklarımızla ilgili ciddi sıkıntılar var. Burada mesela köylerimizde, kırsalda geçici tarım alanlarında yaşayan çocukları izliyor musunuz sistematik olarak? Kaçı için risk tespiti yapıyorsunuz ya da yapılıyor mu? Kaçı için sosyal hizmetlerle koordinasyon kuruluyor, buna ilişkin bir veriniz var mı? Varsa ve atladıysam kusura bakmayın. Şehir merkezlerinde de çocukları korumakta zorluk yaşıyoruz. Doğal olarak kırsalda çocuklar çok daha kaderine terk edilmiş ve zor koşullarda. Özellikle kırsal bölgedeki çocuk işçiliği suça sürüklenmenin en büyük kapısı. Sosyal hizmet uzmanlarının gidemediği yere jandarmamız gidiyor, sizler gidiyorsunuz. Burada çok önemli meselelerden biri de şu: Tarikat ve cemaat yapılanmalarının kırsaldaki kaçak kursları. Buradaki çocuklara yönelik şiddet ve istismar iddialarıyla ilgili -belki konudan bağımsız ama- veri varsa paylaşır mısınız? Ve bu yapılarla ilgili çocukların, bu yapılardan gelen çocukların da suça sürüklenmeleriyle ilgili elinizde herhangi bir veri var mı?
Son soru: Göçmen kaçakçılığı, bir kurum bahsetmişti. Bu çocuklar özelinde elimizde hangi veriler var? Çünkü göçmen çocuklar da suça itiliyor. Onunla ilgili de neler yapılıyor, paylaşırsanız sevinirim.
Teşekkürler.
records.englishTranslation:
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Thank you for your presentations.
Now, really, when do we all see these children of ours, growing up on the streets, living in poverty, with broken families, for the first time? Do we see them in a park, a school, or a social service center, or is the first point of contact the police station? Unfortunately, our answer is the police station. So today, there are tens of thousands of children in the Police records and in your data. I will not explain the data one by one again, but according to your 2024 data, the number of children coming to security units increased by 10 percent in one year and exceeded 600 thousand, I am talking about the number of children. In other words, "We carried out drug operations in front of children's schools - we have them here." You say, but while these children are falling into the arms of drug dealers, gangs like the Daltons, the Barış Boyun Gang, the Redkit, and I don't want to advertise them, are recruiting 15-year-old children from social media as hitmen. My question is: Where is the preventative policing? So, the first of your suggestions here is that it is imperative to strengthen protective and preventive mechanisms. So, what is your preventive policy suggestion as the Police Department? Because the job description of the Police Department cannot only be "catch, bring and deliver". Today, preventive social projects and policies that will keep children away from crime are being talked about in the world - as I just mentioned in the TURKSTAT presentation and gave examples from different countries. Of course, this is not an issue that only concerns our Police Department; in fact, it is something that goes beyond our Police Department and requires political will. That's why I don't want to leave anyone here under suspicion, nor do I want to leave our Police Department or Gendarmerie under suspicion. I have to emphasize that there is a lack of political will here.
Here are a few questions: In your table on page 49 of your police presentation, we see that crimes committed against society increased from 10,307 in 2021 to 17,964 in 2025. The main reason for this terrible increase of nearly 75 percent in five years
what do you think? In other words, "We did these." You give statistics, but ultimately I want to learn from our Police Department their own opinions. Again, in the table above it - your "Crimes Against Persons" table - you now say "There is no increase in 2024-2025, in fact there is a 2 percent decrease." You wrote, but the number of these crimes was 64 thousand in 2021, and increased to 90 thousand in 2025; You only wrote about the decline in the last year here. That's why I want to tell you the truth here so that we can all realize it and that it will be recorded in the minutes.
Now, "We're doing record drug operations on the streets." you say. It is true, you are doing it, but how do you explain the fact that the rate of children falling into this swamp increases exponentially every year? Why can't these drug sales in school areas be stopped? So what are your thoughts and experiences regarding this?
Again, in your report you say that you provided safe internet training to more than 4.4 million people. Well, despite these trainings, illegal betting and crimes against society within the scope of cyber risks are increasing rapidly. What's the reason for this? So, we are providing safe internet training to 4.4 million people, that's great, why is it increasing then?

I mean, you've made a few comments here about the problems in internet use, etc., but - let me say it again, without advertising - I just went out and looked, that is, when you enter the names of those gangs here, when you enter YouTube or any site on the internet, you can see them all. I mean, I'm very sorry, children are recruited and encouraged to be hitmen. Why aren't these sites closed? For example, our Presidential candidate's website can be closed, his social media account can easily be closed, or a young person can be deprived of his freedom for tweeting, or he can be handcuffed behind his back in the middle of the night, but these gangs, these bad advertisements, that is, the topics that encourage this bad crime, continue to be on YouTube and the internet. I wonder what our Police Department is doing about this, or what it can do, what it finds lacking or cannot do? Why can't these criminal machines that encourage children to commit crimes on social media be prevented?
Finally, in your table, there is a nearly 100 percent increase in firearms since 2021. I wonder how these weapons get into the hands of children? Do you have any studies or data on this?
My last question: Increasing trends in missing child cases. To what extent does this put children at risk of falling into the hands of criminal organisations? Do you have any work on this?
Finally, the Gendarmerie is looking after our rural areas, there are serious problems with our children who are dragged into crime in the rural areas. For example, do you systematically monitor children living in temporary agricultural areas in our villages or rural areas? For how many of them do you make risk assessments or are they done? For how many of them are coordinated with social services? Do you have any data on this? Sorry if there is and I missed it. We also have difficulty protecting children in city centres. Naturally, children in rural areas are much more abandoned to their fate and in difficult conditions. Child labor, especially in rural areas, is the biggest gateway to being dragged into crime. Our gendarmerie goes where social workers cannot go, you go. One of the most important issues here is this: Illegal courses of sects and congregations in rural areas. If there is any data regarding the allegations of violence and abuse against children here - perhaps unrelated to the subject - could you please share it? And do you have any data about the children related to these structures and the children coming from these structures being dragged into crime?
Last question: Immigrant smuggling was mentioned by an institution. What data do we have about these children? Because immigrant children are also pushed into crime. I would appreciate it if you could share what is being done about it.
Thanks.
records.englishTitle: On the occasion of the meeting regarding the presentations made by the representatives of the General Directorate of Security and the Gendarmerie General Command.

records.title: TÜİK ve Adalet Bakanlığı temsilcilerinin yaptıkları sunumlara ilişkin görüşme

fields.Subject: TÜİK ve Adalet Bakanlığı temsilcilerinin yaptıkları sunumlara ilişkin görüşme

records.originalTurkish:
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Hoş geldiniz, sunumlarınız için teşekkür ediyoruz.
TÜİK'le başlayacağım, birkaç yorum ve sorum var, ardından Adalet Bakanlığına geçeceğim.
Bir kere öncelikle bir ana muhalefet partisi milletvekili olarak TÜİK'le ilgili zaten tek adam rejimine geçildiğinden beri aslında kurumunuza dair yapılabilecek tüm eleştiriler yapıldı, söylenecek her söz söylendi yani bu kurumun özerk bir kurum olmadığını bir kere daha vurgulamak istiyorum. Burada aslında bir veri mutfağı var, sarayın veri mutfağı; bunu not ederek başlayacağım. Enflasyondan işsizliğe, yoksulluğa, eğitimin verilerine kadar bu verilerin ne hâlde olduğunu vatandaşlarımız da hepimiz de yaşayarak görüyoruz.
Şimdi, çocuklara dönersek verilerinize göre suça sürüklenen çocuk sayısı 2023'te 179 binlerde, 2024'te 202 bine yükseliyor,
yüzde 13'lük bir artış söz konusu ama suça sürüklenen bu çocuklarımızın aile gelir düzeyleri ve ebeveyn işsizliği gibi hayati korelasyonlarını aslında şeffaf bir şekilde yayımladığınızı göremiyoruz. Bunları yayımlarsanız aslında toplumsal sorunun kökenine de inmek mümkün olacak ama bunlar yayımlanmayarak bunun kökenine inilmesi engelleniyor; bunu söylemem lazım.
2023 yılı verilerinize göre güvenlik birimlerine gelen ya da getirilen çocuk sayısı bir önceki yıla oranla yüzde 10 artıyor, 606 binlere ulaşıyor, bu çocukların 242 bin civarı suça sürüklenme nedeniyle geliyor. Sizin verileriniz. Bu artış, aslında yoksulluk ile suç arasındaki doğrudan bir korelasyonun gizlenemez bir sonucu. Türkiye, çocuk yoksulluğunda Avrupa 1'incisi olmuş durumda değerli milletvekilleri. EUROSTAT verilerine göre Türkiye, yüzde 41,3'le Avrupa ülkeleri arasında yoksulluk ve sosyal dışlanma riski altındaki çocuk oranının en yüksek olduğu ülke ve bu oran Avrupa Birliği ortalamasının 2 katı; Avrupa Birliği ortalaması yüzde 24'lerde ve ilginç şekilde, TÜİK'in kendi verilerinde dahi -2023- çocukların yüzde 31,3'ünün şiddetli maddi yoksunluk çeken hanelerde yaşadığını görüyoruz.
Şimdi, bunu niye anlattım? Çünkü biz, suç ile yoksulluk arasında bir bağ olduğunu söylüyoruz, denklem de çok basit; aileyi açlığa, sosyal yardımların insafına mahkûm ederseniz o çocuğun suça sürüklenmesinin önüne de geçmeniz zor; umudu, geleceği yani hayalleri çalınan bu çocuklar hayatta kalma refleksini mafyatik ilişkilerde, uyuşturucu çetelerinde, illegal ilişkilerde arayabiliyorlar ve arıyorlar da. Maalesef, TÜİK de verileri çarpıttıkça biz de bu devasa sorunun köküne inemiyoruz; enflasyondan, işsizlikten... Bu verilere tekrar girmeye gerek yok, veriler gizleniyor, veriler karartılıyor ama bunu yapmaya devam ettikçe bu suçun ortağı olmaya da TÜİK devam ediyor.
Şimdi ben bu soruların bazılarının cevabını biliyorum ama yine de sormak istiyorum: Neden çocukların suça sürüklenme nedenleri ile ailelerin gelir düzeyi, konut durumu ve ebeveyn işsizliği arasındaki korelasyonu şeffaf bir şekilde yayınlamıyorsunuz ya da buna dair bir çalışma neden yapmıyorsunuz? Yaptıysanız açıklayın lütfen. Yine, Millî Eğitim Bakanlığı verilerine göre MESEM kapsamında çalışan öğrenci sayısı 1,5 milyonu aşmış durumda. "MESEM" adı altında maalesef çocuk işçiliği yasallaştırılmış durumda ve bu sistemden kaç çocuk sokağa ve kaç çocuk suça düştü; bununla ilgili bir çalışmanız var mı? Okuldan kopan her çocuk sizin bu istatistiklerinizin bir parçası hâline geliyor ve bu çocukların eğitim durumunu neden ayrıntılı olarak TÜİK de yayınlamıyor? Derin yoksulluk verileriniz nerede? TÜİK'in sefalet sınırını, açlık sınırını "hesaplama yöntemi değişikliği" adı altında gizlemesi aslında çocukların tabağından çalınan ekmeğin üstünü örtmektedir. Bakın, PISA verilerine göre Türkiye'deki öğrencilerin yüzde 19,3'ü para yokluğu nedeniyle haftada en az 1 kez öğün atladığını beyan ediyor. Bu veri, ekonomik krizin doğrudan çocukları gelişim hakkı ve aslında eğitimde fırsat eşitliğinde vurduğunun bir kanıtı. Bugün, Türkiye'de her 5 çocuktan 1'i okulu aç gidiyorsa gerçekten bu çocukların suça sürüklenme riski ne düzeyde etkileniyor acaba? Bunu da hep beraber sormamız lazım.
Dolayısıyla güvenlik birimleriyle ilgili, güvenlik birimlerine gelen çocuk istatistiklerini -verdiniz burada rakamları, internet sitenizde de var ama- neden gecikmeli olarak açıklıyorsunuz, neden zamanında, her yıl başında, yıl sonunda açıklamıyorsunuz ki biz de siyasiler olarak politika önerileri geliştirelim ya da Hükûmet görevini yapsın ve politika geliştirsin?
2024-25 yılı verilerinde çocukların karıştığı suç türlerinde özellikle madde kullanımı, çete bağlantılı suçlarda yaşanan artış neden seyreltiliyor? Bu rakamlar hangi filtrelerden geçirildikten sonra mesela bu Komisyonun önüne geliyor?
Dolayısıyla TÜİK "Yoksulluk düştü." diyor, "Yoksulluk düştü." dedikçe, cezaevlerindeki çocuk koğuşları dolup taşıyor. TÜİK'i bir istatistik kurumu olmaktan çıkarmak, iktidarın veri ofisi hâline getirmekten çıkarmamız gerekiyor çünkü bugün anıldığı gibi de yoksa bu kurum halkın vicdanında "gelecek hırsızı" olarak anılmaya
devam edecek.
Şimdi sunumunuzda 40'ıncı sayfadan başlayalım: Uyuşturucu madde kullanımı ve uyarıcı madde kullanmaktan dolayı işlem yapılan çocuk sayısının bir önceki yıla göre 2 kat arttığını söylüyorsunuz. Şimdi, bu artış belirli bir sosyoekonomik grupta mı yoğunlaşıyor yoksa toplumun geneline yayılan bir yaygınlaşma mı söz konusu? Birincisi bu. Bunu nasıl yorumluyorsunuz?
İkincisi, çocukların en fazla yaralama ve hırsızlık suç birimlerine getirildiğini görüyoruz. Gençlerin şiddete eğilimi ve yoksulluk arasındaki bağ hakkında bu ne anlatıyor yani bununla ilgili ne söylüyorsunuz?
Yine, bahsettiniz göçmenlerden ama sizin verilerinizde suça sürüklenen ya da mağdur olarak belirtilen çocukların ne kadarı göçmen ya da geçici koruma altındaki çocuklar? Bunu da bir açıklamanızı rica edeceğiz.
Yine, uyuşturucuyla ilgili suçlarda görülen 2 katlık artışın göçmen çocuklar arasındaki yansıması nedir? Bunu da bir rica edelim.
Son olarak da "2024'te -sunumunuzda 41'inci sayfa- 279.620 çocuk mağdur olarak güvenlik birimlerine getirilmiştir." deniliyor. Bu mağduriyetin ne kadarı acaba aile içi şiddet, ne kadarı siber zorbalık ya da istismar kaynaklı; buna ilişkin bir veriniz var mı? Varsa lütfen açıklayın ama pek de olduğunu zannetmiyorum. Yoksa da lütfen bununla ilgili çalışma yapın.
TÜİK'le ilgili yorumlarım bu, Adalet Bakanlığa devam edeyim mi? Devam edeyim, peki.
Adalet Bakanlığına gelirsek, aslında Komisyonumuzun da kurulma sebeplerinden biri, kamuoyunda bu suça sürüklenen çocuklar tartışmasını sosyal medyadaki tepkiler sonucu gündeme geldi, hepimizin bildiği gibi, Minguzzi cinayetiyle birlikte yoğun bir şekilde tartışılmaya başlandı. Adalet Bakanlığımızın her zaman yaptığı gibi -Türkiye'de adalet- önce bir olay yaşanıyor, tepkiler büyüyor, kamuoyunda tepkiler olunca iktidar bir düzenleme yapılacağını söylüyor ve Adalet Bakanlığı bu tepkiler üzerine yargı paketleri getiriyor. Yine, hepimiz biliyorduk, on birinci yargı paketinin taslağında bir düzenleme yer aldı suça sürüklenen çocuklarla ilgili, aslında Minguzzi cinayetiyle birlikte bu tartışma yoğun bir şekilde başladı. Neydi bu düzenleme taslakta yer alan? Hatırlayalım: 15-18 yaş arasındaki çocuklara verilen hapis cezalarının artırılmasıyla ilgiliydi. Kamuoyundan tepkiler geldi, sosyal medyada çığ gibi tepkiler büyüyünce bu düzenleme şimdilik paketten çıkartıldı. O yüzden, biz diyoruz ki burada açık bir yaklaşım sorunu vardır; bir taraftan uzmanlar onlarca yıldır, yıllardır "onarıcı adalet" diyor, diğer taraftan Bakanlık "Daha çok ceza, daha çok hapis." diyor. O yüzden, ben çocukları yetişkinlerle aynı mantıkla yargılayan, onları cezaevlerine atan yani aslında kötü koşullarda suça daha da alışır hâle getiren kim? Sormak istiyorum ortaya.
Devam ediyorum.
Hepimiz biliyoruz, organize suç örgütleri, uyuşturucu ticareti, yağma, haraç, kaçakçılık, suçtan elde edilen gelirlerin aklanması, cinayet, yaralama gibi ağır suç alanlarında her geçen gün hem sayısal hem de coğrafi olarak organize suç örgütleri genişliyor, genişlemeye de devam ediyor. Mesela, İngiltere, çeteler tarafından uyuşturucu ve suç için kullanılan çocukları fail olarak değil, mağdur olarak tanımlıyor; polis çocuğu yakalandığında mesela kelepçelemek yerine onu sosyal hizmetlerin koruma şemsiyesi altına alıyor. Bizde ise suç örgütü liderlerinin bir bölümü serbestken çocuklarımız cezaevlerinde ağır cezalarla karşı karşıya kalıyor.
BAŞKAN MÜŞERREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Bilgiye...
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Yo, yo, tabir olarak söyledim, genel olarak. Anladım.
Ülkemizde bu yıl...
BAŞKAN MÜŞERREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Doğru bilgi verelim de. Bilgiye dayalı bir tartışma yürütelim.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Devam edeyim ben, siz cevap verirsiniz Sayın Başkan.
Bu yapılar, bu organize suç çeteleri sosyal medya aracılığıyla -az önce birisi sanırım sunumunda bahsetti- ciddi şekilde
propaganda yapmaya devam ediyor ve bizim yoksulluk ve dışlanmışlık içinde yaşayan mahallelerdeki çocuklarımıza kolayca ulaşıyorlar ve suça yönlendiriyorlar. O yüzden, bu örgütlerin içine çekilen çocuklarımızın, gerçekten taslağınızda düşündüğünüz gibi ağır cezalara mahkûm edilmesi caydırıcı mı, yoksa adalet duygusuyla bağdaşır mı? Bunu da hep beraber sorgulamak lazım Komisyon çalışmaları içinde.
Bir diğer konu, cezaevleri. Biliyorsunuz, bir çocuk hakkında verilen her karar onun tüm hayatını belirliyor diyoruz. Çocuklarla ilgili istatistiklere baktığımızda da görüyoruz, çocuklar resmen adliye koridorlarında yani hapishanede büyür hâle geliyor ne yazık ki. Şimdi, sizin Kasım 2025 verilerinize göre Türkiye'de 4.600 küsur çocuk hapsedilmiş durumda. Bazı çocuklar yetişkin hapishanelerinde de tutuluyor, değil mi? Az önce doğru duydum mu? Öyle bir veriniz de vardı. Ya da tutuluyor mu?
BAŞKAN MÜŞERREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Yaşı 18...
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Biliyorum ama yer olmadığı için. Doğru mu?
ADALET BAKANLIĞI CEZA VE TEVKİFEVLERİ GENEL MÜDÜRÜ ENİS YAVUZ YILDIRIM - Müstakil bölümlerde.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Tamam ama var. Ayrı olduğunu biliyorum ama var mı diye soruyorum.
Şimdi, Yeni Zelanda modelinden... Belki bu modellerde çalışılabilir. Yeni Zelanda modelinde çocuk, hâkim karşısına çıkmadan önce mağdur, aile ve sosyal hizmet uzmanlarıyla bir araya geliyor, ceza yerine çocuğun verdiği zararın nasıl telafi edileceği üzerine bir plan yapılıyor ve Yeni Zelanda, baktığınızda, çocukların cezaevine girme oranını ve mükerrer suç işleme oranını dünya genelinde en düşük seviyelere çeken ülkelerin en başında.
İngiltere ve İrlanda'ya bakıyoruz... Yani bunları niye anlatıyorum? 15-18 yaş arası için cezaları artırmak yerine, dünyadaki onarıcı adalet sistemiyle çocuklarımızı nasıl hapishanelerden çekip çıkarabiliriz, bunları tartışalım diye anlatıyorum. İngiltere ve İrlanda'ya baktığımızda, yine çocuk henüz suçla tanışmadan, 8-11 yaşları arasında risk grubunda tespit ediliyor ve çok paydaşlı bir ağla özellikle belediye, okul ve güvenlik gibi birimlerle hep birlikte destekleniyor yani biz bu ülkede "Cezayı artıralım." derken, dünya -dediğim gibi- "Çocuğu sistemden nasıl uzak tutarız." diye uğraşıyor.
Son örnek, Norveç ve İsveç. Bu ülkelerde de çocuk cezaevleri ve bakımevleri sadece bakanlıklar tarafından denetlenmiyor, bağımsız ve geniş yetkili çocuk ombudsmanları tarafından da denetleniyor.
Dolayısıyla sanık odaklı, cezalandırıcı bir yaklaşımın bir sonucu olarak çocuklar biliyoruz ki hepsi hapishanelerde; istismara maruz kalan, fiziksel, psikolojik şiddete maruz kalan çocuklar var ve bu suçların mağduru olurken diğer yandan da maalesef suç örgütleriyle ilişkilenmelerine zemin hazırlandığı için de fail konumuna geçebiliyorlar. Bunu mağdur ve fail konumunun önemini vurgulamak için söylüyorum.
Az önce bahsettiğim, mesela, bu Norveç ve İsveç'teki çocuk ombudsman denetleme mekanizmaları... Neden mesela bizim çocuk cezaevlerindeki hak ihlalleri bağımsız heyetlerce denetlenmiyor? Böyle bir çalışma var mı? Olabilir.
Suça sürüklenen çocukların büyük bölümü yaralama suçundan geliyor ve bu çocukların kaçı rehabilite edilmiş, kaçı sokağa çıktığında yeniden eline bıçak almış, yeniden suç işlemiş, bununla ilgili sizin bir veriniz var mı? Adli Tıp ve sosyal hizmet entegrasyonu neden kurulamıyor?
Son olarak, son iki soru: Bakanlığınızın sunduğu veriler -şimdi sayfasını bulamadım ama- kurumlar arası -Sayın Başkan da söyledi- protokolleriniz var yani Aile Bakanlığı, Millî Eğitim Bakanlığı arasında "çocukları suçtan uzak tutmak için kurumlar arası koordinasyon" diyorsunuz ama baktığımızda rakamlara, ciddi artışlar da var, bazı dönemler arası azalmalardan bahsediyorsunuz ama bu artışın sorumluluğunu sadece yargı sistemine mi yüklüyorsunuz, yoksa acaba sosyal devlet politikasında da bir eksiklik görüyor musunuz?
Yine, Bakanlığınızın, Millî Eğitim Bakanlığı ile protokolleri çerçevesinde bu MESEM kapsamında çocukların sanayide ucuz iş gücü olarak kullanılmasının önüne geçecek hangi bağımsız denetim mekanizmaları mevcut ya da
böyle bir mekanizmanız var mı? Onu da söylerseniz...
Son sorum da biraz siyasi ama bu çocuk tanımı olan 5395 sayılı Kanun'la 18 yaşını doldurmamış kişiyi çocuk olarak tanımlıyoruz ama MESEM uygulamasıyla bu çocuklara erkenden işçi sorumluluğu yüklüyoruz. Bu kanunun ruhundaki "çocuğun üstün yararı" ilkesiyle sizce çelişmiyor mu?
Teşekkürler.
BAŞKAN MÜŞERREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Şimdi bir söz talebi de var, hemen sayın vekilimize söz vermeden önce küçük bir hatırlatma yapmak istiyorum. Sayın vekilimiz elbette ki çok önemli sorulara, önemli konulara temas etti, önemli katkılarda yapmak ister. Biraz da tabii ki herkes heyecanlı ve bütün soruları bazen siyasi karar ya da... Bu Komisyonun çalışmaları neticesinde ortaya çıkacak veriye dayandıracağımız politika önerilerini "Bunu düşünüyor musunuz, şunu düşünüyor musunuz?" diye sormak için daha başka muhataplar gelecek.

AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Tamam, nasıl konuşacağımızı söylemeyin.

AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Bakın, şöyle yapalım: Bize bilgi notunu yollasınlar, biz onlara soru... Ben zaten soru önergesiyle soruyorum, hiç birine cevap alamıyorum ki bu hâle düşüyoruz.

AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Lütfen, kimseye müdahale etmeyin, rica ediyorum, tarafsız davranın.

EROL KELEŞ (Elâzığ) - Siz konuşurken biz sesimizi çıkarmadık ya! Ya, lütfen ama! Biz mi terk edelim burayı? Saygılı olun lütfen ya!
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Ne dediysem "Bu böyle değil, bu böyle değil."

AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Niye, size de baktım.
records.englishTranslation:
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Welcome, thank you for your presentations.
I will start with TURKSTAT, I have a few comments and questions, then I will move on to the Ministry of Justice.
First of all, as a member of the main opposition party, I would like to emphasize once again that since the transition to a one-man regime regarding TUIK, all possible criticisms of your institution have been made, every word that could be said has been said, that is, this institution is not an autonomous institution. There is actually a data kitchen here, the data kitchen of the palace; I'll start by noting this. From inflation to unemployment, poverty, education data, we, our citizens and all of us, see how these data are.
Now, if we turn to children, according to your data, the number of children dragged into crime will be 179 thousand in 2023, rising to 202 thousand in 2024.
There is a 13 percent increase, but we cannot see that you are actually transparently publishing the vital correlations such as family income levels and parental unemployment of these children who are dragged into crime. If you publish these, it would actually be possible to get to the root of the social problem, but by not publishing them, getting to the root of this is prevented; I have to say this.
According to your data for 2023, the number of children coming or brought to security units increases by 10 percent compared to the previous year, reaching 606 thousand, and around 242 thousand of these children come because of being dragged into crime. Your data. This increase is actually an undisguised consequence of a direct correlation between poverty and crime. Dear MPs, Türkiye ranks first in Europe in terms of child poverty. According to EUROSTAT data, Türkiye is the country with the highest rate of children at risk of poverty and social exclusion among European countries, with 41.3 percent, and this rate is 2 times the European Union average; The European Union average is around 24 percent, and interestingly, even in TÜİK's own data, we see that -2023- 31.3 percent of children live in households suffering from severe financial deprivation.
Now why did I tell you this? Because we say that there is a link between crime and poverty, the equation is very simple; If you condemn the family to hunger and the mercy of social aid, it is difficult to prevent that child from being dragged into crime; These children, whose hope, future, or dreams have been stolen, can and do seek their survival reflex in mafia relationships, drug gangs, and illegal relationships. Unfortunately, as TÜİK distorts the data, we cannot get to the root of this huge problem; from inflation and unemployment. There is no need to re-enter these data, the data is hidden, the data is obscured, but as long as it continues to do this, TÜİK continues to be an accomplice to this crime.
Now I know the answers to some of these questions, but I still want to ask: Why don't you transparently publish the correlation between the reasons why children are dragged into crime and the income level of families, housing status and parental unemployment, or why don't you conduct a study on this? If you did, please explain. Again, according to the data of the Ministry of National Education, the number of students working within the scope of MESEM has exceeded 1.5 million.

Unfortunately, child labor has been legalized under the name of "MESEM" and how many children have fallen into the streets and into crime due to this system; Do you have any work on this? Every child who drops out of school becomes a part of your statistics, and why doesn't TÜİK publish the educational status of these children in detail? Where is your deep poverty data? TurkStat's hiding of the poverty line and hunger line under the name of "change in calculation method" actually covers up the bread stolen from children's plates. Look, according to PISA data, 19.3 percent of students in Türkiye declare that they skip meals at least once a week due to lack of money. This data is proof that the economic crisis directly affects children's right to development and, indeed, equality of opportunity in education. If 1 in 5 children in Türkiye goes to school hungry today, to what extent is the risk of these children being dragged into crime really affected? We need to ask this together.
Therefore, why are you disclosing the statistics regarding the security units, the children coming to the security units - you gave the figures here, they are also on your website - but with a delay, why don't you disclose them on time, at the beginning of each year, at the end of the year, so that we, as politicians, can develop policy suggestions or the Government can do its duty and develop policies?
Why is the increase in the types of crimes involving children, especially substance use and gang-related crimes, diluted in the 2024-25 data? After what filters are these figures passed through, for example, do they come before this Commission?
Therefore, TÜİK said, "Poverty has fallen." “Poverty has fallen,” he says. As I say, children's wards in prisons are overflowing. We need to stop TUIK from being a statistical institution and turning it into the data office of the government, because otherwise this institution will be remembered as the "thief of the future" in the public's conscience.
will continue.
Now, let's start from the 40th page of your presentation: You say that the number of children taken into custody due to drug use and stimulant use has doubled compared to the previous year. Now, is this increase concentrated in a certain socioeconomic group, or is there a spread throughout society? This is the first one. How do you interpret this?
Secondly, we see that children are mostly brought to crime units for injury and theft. What does this say about the link between youth violence and poverty?
Again, you mentioned immigrants, but in your data, how many of the children who are dragged into crime or mentioned as victims are immigrants or children under temporary protection? We would like to ask you to explain this as well.
Again, what is the reflection of the 2-fold increase in drug-related crimes among immigrant children? Let's ask for this too.
Finally, "In 2024 - page 41 of your presentation - 279,620 children were brought to security units as victims." It is said. I wonder how much of this victimization is due to domestic violence, how much of it is due to cyber bullying or abuse; Do you have any data regarding this? If there is, please explain, but I don't think there is much. Otherwise, please work on this.

These are my comments about TURKSTAT, should I continue with the Ministry of Justice? Let me continue, okay.
As for the Ministry of Justice, one of the reasons why our Commission was established was that the public debate about children dragged into crime came to the fore as a result of the reactions on social media. As we all know, it started to be discussed intensively with the Minguzzi murder. As our Ministry of Justice always does - justice in Turkey - first an incident occurs, reactions grow, when there are public reactions, the government says that a regulation will be made and the Ministry of Justice brings judicial packages upon these reactions. Again, we all knew that there was a regulation in the draft of the eleventh judicial package regarding children dragged into crime, in fact, this debate started intensely with the Minguzzi murder. What was this regulation included in the draft? Let's remember: It was about increasing prison sentences for children between the ages of 15-18. There were reactions from the public, and as an avalanche of reactions grew on social media, this regulation was removed from the package for now. Therefore, we say that there is a clear approach problem here; On the one hand, experts have been saying "restorative justice" for decades and years, and on the other hand, the Ministry says "More punishment, more imprisonment." he says. Therefore, I ask, who judges children with the same logic as adults, puts them in prison, that is, actually makes them more accustomed to crime in bad conditions? I want to ask.
I continue.
We all know that organized crime organizations are expanding day by day, both numerically and geographically, in the areas of serious crime such as drug trafficking, looting, extortion, smuggling, laundering of proceeds of crime, murder, and wounding, and they continue to expand. For example, England defines children used by gangs for drugs and crime as victims, not perpetrators; When a child is caught, for example, instead of handcuffing him, the police take him under the protection umbrella of social services. In our country, while some of the leaders of criminal organizations are free, our children face heavy penalties in prisons.
CHAIRMAN MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - For information.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - No, no, I said it as an expression, in general. I understand
This year in our country.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Let's give correct information. Let's have an informed discussion.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Let me continue, you will answer, Mr. President.
These structures, these organized crime gangs, through social media - I think someone just mentioned it in their presentation - seriously
They continue to make propaganda and easily reach our children in neighborhoods living in poverty and exclusion and direct them to crime. Therefore, is it really a deterrent or compatible with the sense of justice if our children who are drawn into these organizations are sentenced to heavy penalties as you think in your draft? We need to question this together within the Commission's work.
Another issue is prisons. You know, we say that every decision made about a child determines his or her entire life.

When we look at the statistics about children, we see that, unfortunately, children are literally growing up in the corridors of the courthouse, that is, in prison. Now, according to your November 2025 data, more than 4,600 children are imprisoned in Türkiye. Some children are also held in adult prisons, right? Did I just hear that correctly? You also had such data. Or is it kept?
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Age 18.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - I know, but because there is no space. Is it true?
MINISTRY OF JUSTICE GENERAL DIRECTOR OF PRISON AND DETENTION HOUSES ENIS YAVUZ YILDIRIM - In individual departments.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - OK, but there is. I know it's separate, but I'm asking if it exists.
Now, from the New Zealand model. Maybe these models can be worked on. In the New Zealand model, the child meets with the victim, family and social workers before appearing before the judge, a plan is made on how to compensate for the damage caused by the child instead of punishment, and when you look at it, New Zealand is at the top of the countries that reduce the rate of children going to prison and the rate of repeat crime to the lowest levels in the world.
We're looking at the UK and Ireland. So why am I telling these? I am explaining this so that we can discuss how we can take our children out of prisons with the restorative justice system around the world, instead of increasing the penalties for those aged 15-18. When we look at the UK and Ireland, again, the child is identified in the risk group between the ages of 8-11, before he even encounters crime, and is supported by a multi-stakeholder network, especially by units such as the municipality, school and security. In this country, we say, "Let's increase the punishment." Then the world - as I said - asks "How can we keep the child away from the system?" he tries.
The last example is Norway and Sweden. In these countries, juvenile prisons and care homes are not only inspected by ministries, but also by independent and broadly authorized juvenile ombudsmen.
Therefore, as a result of a defendant-focused, punitive approach, we know that children are all in prisons; There are children who are abused, exposed to physical and psychological violence, and while they are victims of these crimes, unfortunately, they can also become perpetrators because the ground is prepared for their association with criminal organizations. I say this to emphasize the importance of the victim and perpetrator position.
What I just mentioned, for example, these child ombudsman monitoring mechanisms in Norway and Sweden. Why, for example, are not human rights violations in our juvenile prisons inspected by independent committees? Is there such a study? It could be.
Most of the children who are dragged into crime come from the crime of wounding. How many of these children have been rehabilitated, how many of them have picked up a knife again when they went out on the streets, and how many have committed crimes again? Do you have any data on this? Why can't the integration of Forensic Medicine and social services be established?

Finally, the last two questions: The data presented by your Ministry - I couldn't find the page now, but - Mr. President also said - you have inter-institutional protocols, that is, you say "inter-institutional coordination to keep children away from crime" between the Ministry of Family and the Ministry of National Education, but when we look at the figures, there are also serious increases, you talk about decreases between some periods, but do you put the responsibility for this increase only on the judicial system, or do you see a deficiency in the social state policy as well?
Again, within the scope of this MESEM, within the framework of your Ministry's protocols with the Ministry of National Education, what independent control mechanisms do you have or do you have to prevent children from being used as cheap labor in the industry?
Do you have such a mechanism? If you say that too.
My last question is a bit political, but with Law No. 5395, which is the definition of child, we define a person who has not reached the age of 18 as a child, but with the MESEM application, we impose worker responsibilities on these children at an early age. Don't you think this contradicts the "best interest of the child" principle in the spirit of the law?
Thanks.
PRESIDENT MUŞARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Now there is a request for a speech, I would like to make a small reminder to our esteemed deputy before giving the floor. Our esteemed representative has of course touched on very important questions and important issues, and he would like to make important contributions. Of course, everyone is excited and all their questions are sometimes related to political decisions or issues. We will base the policy recommendations on the data that will emerge as a result of the work of this Commission by asking "Do you think about this, do you think about that?" More interlocutors will come to ask.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - We decide how we want to talk, that is, we talk what we want.
PRESIDENT MUŞARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Of course, you can talk as you want, but there is also a situation like this.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - So this is not a university. This is a political place.
CHAIRMAN MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - The purpose of this meeting.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - I know the purpose of the meeting, and I decide how I want to talk.
EROL KELEŞ (Elâzığ) - If you allow him, let him talk. Look, nobody said anything while you were talking.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - I can talk as I want.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - This is not a debate club.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - I can talk as I want, I cannot ask you how to talk.
EROL KELEŞ (Elâzığ) - But please do not intervene.
PRESIDENT MUSARRAF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - But I will say something.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - I really don't want to make a polemic.
EROL KELEŞ (Elâzığ) - Should we have answered too!
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - But let us decide how we will talk.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - We cannot get results without methodology, so this is not teaching, it is scientific.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - We decide how we want to talk, otherwise we will leave.
PRESIDENT MUŞERREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - You know, the scientific thought that Atatürk suggested to us, that is exactly the scientific thought that will raise it above the level of contemporary civilization, we can produce benefits for children by working with a certain methodology.

I find the questions you ask very valuable, but the addressee.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - That's why I said, we would be more comfortable if he was the Minister of Justice.
PRESIDENT MUSHARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - I have already told you that the Minister of Justice will come, I have already told you that the Minister of Justice will come. But in the methodology, we said that the first two weeks will analyze the risk factors and the situation.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Shouldn't we talk? Then, let's go and let them report to us.
ALİ İNCİ (Sakarya) - For God's sake, do not use this word!
CHAIRMAN MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Oh, speak, but Mr. Deputy, speak. Dear Deputy, I can listen to you for hours, what do you mean?
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Okay, don't tell us how to talk.
ALİ İNCİ (Sakarya) - Well, we are here too.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - I'm asking you.
ALİ İNCİ (Sakarya) - But there is no need to listen there.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - How we talk and how we ask questions.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - I would like to request you to use your time economically and try to comply with the methodology as much as possible.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Look, let's do it like this: Let them send us the information note, we will ask them questions. I am already asking questions through a motion, but I cannot get an answer to any of them, and we are in this situation.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - You are not asking your interlocutor right now.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Well, okay, then they shouldn't come, why are we here if there is no addressee? Don't do it, I beg you!
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - All your interlocutors will come within the framework of a methodology, we told you.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Well, let's do it this way: Give us which questions we should ask, in which methodology.
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Give the questions too.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Now, okay, just a little reminder.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Let them prepare the answers, so let's do it that way.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Dear Deputy, please.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - So you are making me do something. I'm asking you!
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - But you are also interfering with our rights. I made a request to move forward in a method circle, there is nothing personally about you here.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Please, do not interfere with anyone, I beg you, act impartially.
PRESIDENT MUŞARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Impartiality actually requires sticking to the framework.
GİZEM ÖZCAN (Muğla) - In order to close this issue and contribute, I will say this.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - I will say one more sentence, then I will speak in order.
GİZEM ÖZCAN (Muğla) - OK.
PRESIDENT MUŞARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - This is not a one-man system, it is a Presidential government system.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - That's what we say, you can fix it.
PRESIDENT MUŞARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - And our President was elected with over 50 percent of the votes. So, this is not a place for political polemics, nor is it a debate club.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Now, me.
EROL KELEŞ (Elâzığ) - Dear Deputy, please but, please but. Let him finish his sentence, please.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - He corrects whatever I said.

EROL KELEŞ (Elâzığ) - We didn't raise our voices while you were talking! Oh, please! Should we leave this place? Be respectful please!
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Whatever I said, "This is not like this, this is not like that."
MAYOR MUŞARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - I have endless respect and love for you, but we would be doing an injustice to our children by discussing a political issue here. We gathered with another issue.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - The Grand National Assembly of Türkiye is a political place.
ALİ İNCİ (Sakarya) - You turn on the screen, you talk to the screen as if we are not here, and this is not a nice thing.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Why, I looked at you too.

records.title: Emniyet Genel Müdürlüğü ve Jandarma Genel Komutanlığı temsilcilerinin yaptıkları sunumlara ilişkin görüşme münasebetiyle

fields.Subject: Emniyet Genel Müdürlüğü ve Jandarma Genel Komutanlığı temsilcilerinin yaptıkları sunumlara ilişkin görüşme münasebetiyle

records.originalTurkish:
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Teşekkürler sunum ve cevaplarınız için de bir şeyi söylemek istedim: Polis, Jandarma devletimizin polisi, devletimizin jandarması yani biz "Hükûmetin polisi, Hükûmetin jandarması" öyle demiyoruz zaten.
EMNİYET GENEL MÜDÜR YARDIMCISI MUSTAFA ÇALIŞKAN - Öyle bir şey demedim.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Ben size cevap vermiyorum, kayıtlara geçmesi açısından. Yani iktidarlar gelir geçer, devletimiz, polisimiz bakidir. Sizler de elbette ki politikaları uygulamakla yükümlüsünüz, bunun farkında olarak bazı yorumları yaptık; birincisi bu yani sizlere kıymet veriyoruz ancak
önemli olan şeylerden biri şu: Bu Komisyonun fayda sağlayabilmesi için çok ciddi verilere ihtiyacımız var. Tabii, siz burada hepsine cevap veremezsiniz, erişebilir olmuyor olabilirsiniz ancak bunların bazılarını da tabii yuvarlak geçmemek de lazım. Bizim bu verilere Komisyonun raporu ve bazı şeyleri açıklamak için ihtiyacımız var. Onları biz yazılı olarak alalım, burada dile getirdiklerimizi birinci olarak. İkinci olarak soru setlerimizi eksik kalmaması bakımından biz yazılı olarak ileteceğiz. Bunlar tutanağa geçsin diye anlatıyorum. O yazılı sorularımıza gelecek yazılı cevapları da Komisyon üyesi milletvekilinin e-posta hesaplarına özel olarak rica edeceğiz. Bunları yine tutanaklara geçmesi açısından söylüyorum çünkü ben çok defalarca araştırma komisyonlarında görev yaptım, bazı komisyonlarda sorulara gelen cevapları sekreterya arkadaşlar üyelere bazen aktarmayı atlayabiliyorlar. Dolayısıyla bunun önemini usulen burada söylüyorum. O veriler çok önemli yani hani o verilere... Bazı veriler belki paylaşılmamak üzere bu yasama organına iletiliyor olmalı, onu söyleyecektim.
Unuttuğum bir şey varsa da teşekkür ediyoruz tekrardan.
BAŞKAN MÜŞERREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Çok teşekkür ediyoruz. Uyarılarınızı dikkate alacaktır uzman arkadaşlarımız da.
EMNİYET GENEL MÜDÜR YARDIMCISI MUSTAFA ÇALIŞKAN - Sayın Başkanım, bu bizim açımızdan yanlış olabilir. Biz Komisyona yazmamız gerekiyor.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Biz Komisyon üyesi milletvekillerine de gönderebiliyorsunuz.
BAŞKAN MÜŞERREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Siz, şeye bakarız, biz...
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Gönderebiliyorlar. Daha önce de başımıza geldi yani Komisyon sekretaryamızı tenzih ederek söylüyorum, Amasra maden faciası yaşandı 2021'de, belki hatırlarsınız, çok ciddi, her toplantının en az sekiz saat sürdüğü Komisyon çalışmalarına dört ay katıldık ve sorduğumuz sorular sekretaryaya bazen iletildi, hoşlarına gitmeyenler bize iletilmedi; almak istiyorsun, gelmedi cevap. Yani bunu buradaki arkadaşlarımı ve mevkidaşlarımı tenzih ederek söylüyorum; kayıtlara geçsin diye ben, tutanaklara girsin diye söyledim. Bu çok önemli bir şey, sürekli yaşadığımız bir şey, size garip geliyor olabilir, devlette hani istenilir, verilir; burada öyle olmuyor.
records.englishTranslation:
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - Thank you, I wanted to say one thing for your presentation and answers: Police, Gendarmerie is the police of our state, the gendarmerie of our state, that is, we do not say "government's police, government's gendarmerie" like that anyway.
DEPUTY GENERAL MANAGER OF POLICE MUSTAFA ÇALIŞKAN - I didn't say anything like that.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - I am not answering you, for the record. In other words, governments come and go, our state and police are eternal. Of course, you are responsible for implementing the policies, we made some comments while being aware of this; This is the first one, we value you, but
One of the important things is this: We need very serious data for this Commission to benefit. Of course, you cannot answer all of them here, you may not be able to access them, but of course, some of them should not be mentioned. We need this data for the Commission's report and to explain some things. We will get them in writing, first of all what we have expressed here. Secondly, we will submit our question sets in writing to ensure that they are not incomplete. I'm telling you this so that it can be recorded. We will also request written answers to our written questions privately to the e-mail accounts of the Members of the Commission. I am saying these again for the sake of being included in the minutes because I have served in research commissions many times, and in some commissions, secretariat friends can sometimes skip conveying the answers to the questions to the members. Therefore, I am stating the importance of this here. That data is very important, you know, those data... I was going to say that some data should be forwarded to this legislative body, perhaps not to be shared.
Even if I forgot something, thank you again.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - Thank you very much. Our expert friends will also take your warnings into consideration.
DEPUTY GENERAL DIRECTOR OF POLICE MUSTAFA ÇALIŞKAN - Mr. President, this may be wrong for us. We need to write to the Commission.
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - You can also send it to us, members of the Commission.
PRESIDENT MUSARREF PERVİN TUBA DURGUT - You, we will look at the thing, we...
AYSU BANKOĞLU (Bartın) - They can send it. It has happened to us before, I mean, with the exception of our Commission secretariat, the Amasra mining disaster occurred in 2021, you may remember, it is very serious, we participated in the Commission works for four months, where each meeting lasted at least eight hours, and the questions we asked were sometimes forwarded to the secretariat, those we did not like were not forwarded to us; You want to receive it, but the answer did not come. I say this with the exception of my friends and colleagues here; I said it so that it would be on the record, so that it would be included in the minutes. This is a very important thing, it is something we experience all the time, it may seem strange to you, in the state it is asked and given; That doesn't happen here.
